title: Recommendation: tracing + retry behavior for next demo run
summary: Keep claim-level trace summaries in the UI and store full raw traces on disk; cap retries tightly (1 auto retry only for transient provider errors) with explicit manifest logging. This preserves reviewer value while staying within the 1.15x cost ceiling and maintaining live-demo responsiveness.

claims.text: Candidate prompt increases median token usage by ~11% but improves latency by ~6% due to fewer manual reruns (not shorter generations).

Finance will accept up to a 1.15x cost ratio for demo-quality runs if extra tokens are justified by reduced review time; above that requires smaller batches, stricter retry caps, or more aggressive trace summarization.
Reviewers find long traces useful only when summarized next to the failing claim; raw provider payloads slow review because evidence is hard to locate.
Live demo felt responsive when a single briefing completes under 10 seconds and progress shows the current case; longer full runs are acceptable if progress/artifact paths update continuously.
Draft policy: allow one automatic retry for transient provider errors; require a manifest entry when retries change cost/latency; avoid silent repeated generations.

openQuestions: Do we have a reliable way to classify 'transient provider errors' vs. model quality failures for the one allowed auto-retry?
Is the next demo a single-briefing live flow, a full batch eval, or both (to tune the 10s target vs. batch cost constraints)?
Are we currently exceeding the 1.15x cost ratio when enabling full raw payload retention in the dashboard (as opposed to disk-only)?
What fields are required in the manifest for retry/cost explanation during stakeholder review?
recommendation: Tracing: Keep claim-level trace summaries visible in the dashboard (selected source ids, what evidence was missing, retry count, and output/artifact path), and keep detailed raw traces/payloads on disk only (not dumped into the main UI). This matches reviewer preferences and keeps the live demo readable. Retries: Use a strict cap—1 automatic retry only for transient provider errors; no silent repeated generations. Log any retry occurrence and its cost/latency impact in a manifest. Cost/latency guardrails: Target staying within the 1.15x cost ceiling; if projected above, first tighten retries (already capped), then reduce batch size and/or summarize traces more aggressively rather than expanding raw trace retention in the UI. Demo responsiveness: Prioritize sub-10s completion for individual briefings; for longer eval runs, ensure continuous progress indicators (current case, elapsed time, artifact path updates).
